name: Stackbit Visual Editor for Jamstack API
what_it_does: Visual editor layer for Jamstack sites with API — enabling development teams to add a no-code visual editing experience on top of any headless CMS (Contentful, Sanity, Agility, Netlify CMS) and any static site generator (Next.js, Gatsby, Hugo), letting marketing teams edit content visually while developers keep full code control.
use_cases: Adding visual editing to Jamstack sites via Stackbit SDK for content editor experience
Integrating Stackbit with existing headless CMS via API for visual preview capabilities
Managing Stackbit projects and team access via API for agency multi-client management
Publishing Stackbit content changes via API for content workflow automation
Connecting Stackbit to custom data sources via API for non-standard CMS integrations
not_for: Non-Jamstack sites (Stackbit is designed for static/Jamstack architecture)
Teams without developer setup (requires SDK integration in codebase)
Teams looking for a standalone CMS (Stackbit is a layer on top of existing CMSs)
best_when: A dev agency or marketing team wants to give non-technical editors a visual editing experience on their existing Jamstack site — without rebuilding the CMS or compromising developer workflow.
avoid_when: You don't have a Jamstack setup, need a full CMS replacement, or lack developer resources to integrate the SDK.

auth.notes: API token from Stackbit dashboard. OAuth2 for CMS integrations (Contentful, Sanity OAuth). Bearer token auth. Webhook for content and deploy events. JavaScript/TypeScript SDK (@stackbit/sdk). Per-project tokens. Integration with headless CMS OAuth flows. No fine-grained scopes per endpoint. Netlify identity integration for user auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Hobby plan: 1 project, 1 user, community support
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $49/month (5 projects, 5 editors); Pro $149/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Tel Aviv company. Founded 2018 by Ohad Eder-Pressman and Uzi Paz. Raised $15M Series A (2021) from Bessemer. Acquired by Netlify (2023). Now Netlify's visual editor product. Post-acquisition — integrated into Netlify platform. SDK-based visual editing: developers annotate components, editors can modify via visual UI. CMS-agnostic: works with Contentful, Sanity, Agility, Dato, and others. SSG-agnostic: Next.js, Gatsby, Hugo, Astro support. Netlify acquisition provides strong hosting integration.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API + JS/TS SDK. API token auth. Webhook for content events. Netlify acquisition may change API roadmap. SDK required in codebase for visual editing. SOC2 compliant. Multiple OAuth connections to underlying CMSs add complexity. Rate limits not documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads idempotent by ID. Content reads delegated to underlying CMS (depends on CMS). Deploy triggers not idempotent. SDK annotations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: SDK integration required in codebase — not a standalone API for content management
Acquired by Netlify (2023) — product roadmap may change with Netlify integration
Connects to underlying CMS via OAuth — requires CMS credentials management
Jamstack-only — requires static site generator setup
Rate limits not documented — test before high-frequency operations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 compliant. Bearer token auth. OAuth2 for CMS integrations. Netlify ownership provides enterprise security focus. Multiple CMS OAuth connections create larger credential footprint. SDK annotations in code are public — no secrets in annotations. US-only data residency (Netlify infrastructure).
